---
pagetitle: "Home"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.top-logo-section}
![](images/logo/acfe-logo-horizontal2.png)
:::

::: {.hero-section}

::: {.hero-text}
<!--
# Advanced Computational Flow and Energy Lab
-->
### High-performance computing, and data-driven methods for complex flow and energy systems

The Advanced Computational Flow and Energy Lab at North Carolina A&T State University develops computational methods for studying complex fluid flows, turbulence, energy transport, and environmental flow systems.

:::

<div class="hero-image">
  <video autoplay loop muted playsinline>
    <source src="videos/image36-small.mp4" type="video/mp4">
    Your browser does not support the video tag.
  </video>
</div>

<!--
::: {.hero-image}
![](images/research/image36.gif)
:::
-->

:::

::: {.content-section}

## About the Lab

The Advanced Computational Flow and Energy Lab's research lies at the intersection of fluid mechanics, scientific computing, and data-driven modeling. We develop and apply computational tools to improve the understanding and prediction of complex flow systems in engineering, energy, and environmental applications.

Our research focuses on high-fidelity simulation, reduced-order modeling, machine learning for computational fluid dynamics, wind energy, atmospheric and environmental flows, and fire-atmosphere interactions.

[Learn more about our research](research.qmd){.primary-button}

## Affiliation

Department of Mechanical Engineering <br>
North Carolina A&T State University

:::
